evidence_quotes.quote: cancellation fees from subscription services that lead you into a monthly subscription with a yearly agreement.
reasonably functional access to purchased digital goods/games after companies have ended support

paragraphs.text: Having these avenues to easily receive feedback is impressive and greatly appreciated! I'm coming from the interest of videogame preservation initiative, but mainly will point out 2 practices that are concerning. I'm interested in having explicit outlines or removal of cancellation fees from subscription services that lead you into a monthly subscription with a yearly agreement. I'd love to also see if there were inclusions in this Act for having reasonably functional access to purchased digital goods/games after companies have ended support or forwarding of the ''Stop Destroying Videogames'' initiative. Having companies that are planning to creating a product in the future also devise an end-of-life plan. The digital landscape involves other concerns - Cryptocurrency legitimacy, payment processors pressuring digital marketplaces and AI model training data sources, but I am unsure how actionable these broader subjects are. I will only mention them.
